description: This multi-panel figure illustrates the transition from wavy to planar dislocation slip in Fe-Mn-C-Al low-density steels with increasing aluminum content and its effect on work hardening. Panel (a) plots the parameter $W_R$ (×10⁻⁴ m) versus Al content (wt.%), showing a linear decrease from approximately 29 at 0% Al to 10 at 6% Al, which predicts the slip mode transition. Subfigures (a1) and (a2) are TEM micrographs (scale bar 200 nm) comparing Fe-22Mn-3Al and Fe-22Mn-6Al alloys strained to ε=0.1, revealing a change from dislocation cells (DCs) characteristic of wavy slip to planar slip bands. Panel (b) presents an ECCI micrograph (scale bar 1 μm) showing narrow, dense coplanar slip microbands at ε=0.14. Panel (c) displays true stress and strain hardening rate curves versus true strain, demonstrating the SRO-enhanced work hardening behavior. Collectively, the figure provides evidence that Al addition promotes short-range order (SRO), facilitating planar slip and microband formation, which significantly contributes to the work hardening capacity of these steels.
